title: Arivela linophylla
intro: Arivela linophylla, also known as Arivela, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. Arivela linophylla has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects. The bioactive constituents of Arivela linophylla include essential oils, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been identified to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Arivela linophylla include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
taxonomy: Arivela linophylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. The Gesneriaceae family is part of the order Lamiales, which comprises a diverse group of flowering plants. Lamiales is a larger clade that includes over 24,000 species of flowering plants. Arivela linophylla is a species within this larger clade.
